free_text: C-14(d,n)N-15 reaction.
Neutron spectrometer was used. A 1 cm diameter,
0.46 mm thick polyethylene radiator was supported in
the hemisphere by a thin wire. The detector was a 1.9
cm diameter, 1.4 mm thick CsI crystal mounted on a Du
Mont 6291 photomultiplier tube.
Thin carbon targets were prepared by a discharge in
C2H2 gas which deposited carbon on the wolfram backing.
For the C-14 target, 38% enriched C-14 was used. The
thickness of the target was determined by weighing to
be approximately 0.1 mg/cm2.

Fig.8
Neutron spectrometer was used. A 1 cm diameter,
0.46 mm thick polyethylene radiator was supported in
the hemisphere by a thin wire. The detector was a 1.9
cm diameter, 1.4 mm thick CsI crystal mounted on a Du
Mont 6291 photomultiplier tube.
Thin carbon targets were prepared by a discharge in
C2H2 gas which deposited carbon on the wolfram backing.
For the C-14 target, 38% enriched C-14 was used. The
thickness of the target was determined by weighing to
be approximately 0.1 mg/cm2.
No explanation of relative units is given.
The "slow" neutron detector was the usual
enriched BF3 counter with a small paraffin cylinder
(4.5 cm o.d. by 4 cm long) surrounding. The counter
was covered by 0.8 mm cadmium sheet to reduce the
thermal neutron background.
Thin oxygen targets were prepared by heating wolfram
disks in oxygen at ~ 1 cm pressure.

Fig.11
No explanation of relative units is given.
A mixture of high viscosity silicone fluid and
Ag activated ZnS powder (1/3 by volume) mounted on an
RCA 5819 photomultiplier tube was used as the detector
for the fast neutrons.
Thin oxygen targets were prepared by heating wolfram
disks in oxygen at ~ 1 cm pressure.
